summary: sensivity ships obfuscated game-cheat payload with disguised persistence and HWID exfiltration
details: On require() or CLI invocation, launcher.js installs an HKCU Run key named 'OneDriveUpdate' pointing at OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs, which silently re-launches `node launcher.js` hidden via wscript — a Microsoft-lookalike persistence entry that survives reboot. launcher.js then reads server.obf.js (a 171 KB obfuscator.io-packed blob with RC4-decoded string arrays) and eval()s it. The obfuscated server loads sens.node, a 6.5 MB native binary whose strings ('Enable FL', 'Freecam', 'Teleport', 'LeftFoot', 'LeftHand', 'Head', '__licenseAccepted') and FiveM/GTA-V process attachment (getFiveMProcessName) identify it as a Roblox/FiveM game cheat. The server builds a hardware fingerprint from HKLM\...\Cryptography\MachineGuid (read via PowerShell), os.hostname(), and the C: volume serial (WMIC), SHA-256-hashes the join, and POSTs it with a nonce to an obfuscated LICENSE_URL; responses are RSA-verified against an embedded public key. To evade detection, launcher.js sets process.title to 'Runtime Broker', filters log lines containing 'program module loaded', rewrites 'sens.node' to 'program module' and APP_DIR to '<app>' in console output, and spawns the worker detached with windowsHide:true. The combination of disguised autostart persistence, eval of a heavily obfuscated payload, a native cheat module, hardware-identifier exfiltration to an author-controlled endpoint, and process/log masquerading is unambiguously malicious.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
